summary: The diff implements most requirements: Dashboard and Admin NGO Approval routes/links are removed (REQ-29, REQ-31), FoodListings has Accept/Mark as Collected action buttons with status badges and toast notifications in the frontend (REQ-33 frontend portion), Home page has only Donate Food and Get Help CTAs with no stats counters (REQ-35), and backend has DonationStatus type and related functions. However, REQ-32 is partially violated — Navigation includes 'Food Listings' as a 6th link, which may be acceptable under REQ-34 conflict resolution, but the nav does not show 'exactly' the 5 links specified. REQ-33 backend updateDonationStatus function requirement needs scrutiny. REQ-30 AC says /listings route is removed, but it's kept (justified by REQ-34).

missing_requirements.requirement: Navigation shows exactly: Home, Donate, Get Help, Volunteer, NGO Register. All other nav links must be removed.
missing_requirements.reason: Navigation.tsx includes 'Food Listings' as a 6th link, giving 6 nav items instead of exactly 5. While REQ-34 provides a conditional exception, REQ-32 still specifies exactly those 5 links. The nav now shows Home, Donate Food, Get Help, Food Listings, Volunteer, NGO Register.

missing_requirements.requirement: Backend exposes an updateDonationStatus function that accepts a donation ID and new status string.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend adds acceptDonation and markDonationAsCollected functions instead of a unified updateDonationStatus function accepting a donation ID and new status string as required by the acceptance criteria.

hallucinated_features.description: A migration module (backend/migration.mo) was added to handle schema migration from an old actor state to a new one with foodDonations list.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request did not mention adding a migration module or migrating existing backend state. This is an implementation detail added beyond the specified requirements.

hallucinated_features.description: A urlParams utility module (frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts) was added with functions for URL parameter parsing, session storage persistence, and admin token handling.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request did not request any URL parameter utility, session storage utilities, or admin token URL parameter handling. This is not mentioned in any requirement.
